hardware_profile.name: gemmini_micro_v1
hardware_profile.description: Right-sized micro tensor accelerator for 64x64 bf16 matmul. 8x8 systolic mesh (64 MACs), 16-lane vector units, 128 B/cycle DMA. Derived from toy_npu_mvp_v1 by 4x linear scaling.
hardware_profile.notes: All energy_per_op/byte rates are identical to toy_npu_mvp_v1 (same technology). Area, power, throughput scaled 4x down. SPAD=[128,64] bf16 = 16KB. ACC=[64,64] bf16 = 8KB. Total logic area ~1.42 mm2, memory area ~0.24 mm2.

tensor_compute.calibration_target: Operand/scratchpad byte energy aligned to sram_energy_per_byte_pj for matmul dot/gemm proxy.

tensor_compute_tiled.calibration_target: Operand/scratchpad byte energy aligned to sram_energy_per_byte_pj for matmul dot/gemm proxy.

onchip_compute.calibration_target: ADD_16b unit cell x16 lanes (4x down from 64)

activation_tag.calibration_target: ADD_16b unit cell x16 lanes (4x down from 64)

onchip_movement.calibration_target: Presentation-safe Sky130 SRAM/SPAD byte proxy: 19.0 pJ/B. This is still a byte proxy, not an isolated Gemmini SPAD IP measurement.

offchip_movement.calibration_target: Preliminary matmul: VRAM/HBM-style byte energy 60 pJ/B (see memory_energy_pj_per_byte).

migration_notes.details: Migrated from abstraction_classes to cost_tags for realization-first estimator. Legacy abstraction-class configs are still accepted by estimator compatibility logic.

gemmini_schedule_event_model.notes: V3 Gemmini schedule expansion: charge explicit SPAD/local/on-chip movement event bytes as onchip_movement at 19.0 pJ/byte, a Sky130 SRAM/SPAD byte proxy. This is intentionally not a full SPAD hierarchy model; it does not include SPAD active-window/control/local-clock overhead. DMA command counts are expanded per 8x8 tile, and GEMM/MAC symbolic loads are expanded to match the current full-chip tiled probe, which reloads operands for each compute tile pass. Accumulator-prefixed bytes are excluded when gemmini_accumulator_event_model is enabled so ACC activity is not double counted. External offchip_read/write_bytes are intentionally excluded because full-chip PrimeTime does not model external DRAM/HBM energy.

gemmini_accumulator_event_model.source: isolated ACC read/write/RMW PrimeTime sweep summary
gemmini_accumulator_event_model.method: PrimeTime total power integrated over VCS SAIF window; event coefficient is the fitted slope of activity-mode energy minus matching idle energy on the same routed ACC netlist across WORKLOAD_TILES=1,16,256. The corrected activity sweep normalizes by valid-cycle 32B chunk counts and uses sequential read then write phases for RMW because same-cycle read/write to one ACC address is unsupported by AccumulatorMem.

active_envelope.method: IP-derived clocked envelope. Uses matching idle ACC IP power times clock period as pJ/active-cycle, then adds read/write/RMW idle-subtracted data-toggle coefficients separately.

gemmini_accumulator_event_model.notes: Isolated ACC delta model from corrected idle/read/write/RMW SAIFs on one routed ACC IP, fit across 1/16/256-tile activity sweeps. The current routed wrapper does not expose ready signals, so the activity-only rerun counts valid active-cycle chunks rather than true ready/valid fires. True fire-based calibration requires a wrapper change and physical rerun. The active_envelope action is recorded as a separate IP-derived clocked active-cycle term; read/write/RMW coefficients remain idle-subtracted data-toggle terms.

gemmini_locked_bf16_isolated_ip_calibrat.acc_compute_note: Accumulator activity is now modeled by gemmini_accumulator_event_model using an isolated-IP idle-subtracted read/write/RMW slope fit.

memory_energy_pj_per_byte.notes: VRAM/HBM-style traffic: 60 pJ/byte (GDDR6 class ~7.5 pJ/bit). On-chip SRAM/SPAD byte proxy: 19.0 pJ/byte for Sky130 presentation comparisons. Applied via cost_tags offchip_movement / onchip_movement / tensor_compute energy_per_byte_pj. This is not a full SPAD active-window/control model.
references: O'Connor MICRO 2017; Chatterjee HPCA 2017 (DRAM/GPU memory energy)
